tech.note: San Luis Obispo adopted electronic pollbooks for the FIRST TIME at the June 2, 2026 Statewide Direct Primary -- the county had been a paper-roster holdout. County Clerk-Recorder Elaina Cano: 'we're one of the few counties left that don't use [e-pollbooks]' (KSBY, 'SLO County introduces new tech for upcoming elections'). Deputy Director/Registrar Penny Boyd (KSBY, 'New E-poll books coming to San Luis Obispo County polling locations'): e-pollbooks replace the paper rosters voters signed at each polling location, give poll workers real-time access to voter registration to confirm a voter is registered and at the correct assigned polling place, save ~$100,000/yr in roster printing, and cut provisional ballots. adopted_year=2026 = first election conducted with the tech (June 2, 2026). VENDOR not yet publicly disclosed in coverage; the only SoS-certified e-pollbooks in CA are KNOWiNK PollPad and Tenex Precinct Central, so SLO's product is almost certainly one of those, but neither news nor a primary procurement doc naming it was located. The CA SoS 'Voting Technologies in Use by County' (as of Oct 10, 2025) still lists SLO's e-pollbook column as 'Do not use', consistent with the e-pollbook being brand-new for 2026 and post-dating that list. VERIFIED: the evidence_url KSBY article confirms e-pollbooks are used 'for the first time' at the June 2 Primary and quotes Clerk-Recorder Cano ('We're one of the few counties left that don't use [e-pollbooks]'); adopted_year=2026 = first-election year is correct. The article does NOT quote Penny Boyd and names NO vendor (the Boyd detail above comes from a separate KSBY piece, not this evidence_url). Confidence downgraded primary->secondary because adoption is supported ONLY by KSBY news; no official registrar/SoS adoption doc exists yet (the Oct 2025 SoS voting-tech list still shows SLO e-pollbook as 'Do not use').

FIRMED UP (2026-07-09, Task 12): the county's own official 'Signature "Cure" Letters' page (evidence_url, verified by curl fetch) states: 'The following information is provided pursuant to EC section 3019 regarding how to "cure" noncomparable and missing signatures on ballot identification envelopes,' i.e. SLO runs the standard statutory human-cure workflow (Elections Code 3019), not a description of any automated signature-matching product. Independently, KSBY's 'San Luis Obispo County introduces new tech for upcoming elections' (the same article cited for the epollbook row above; verified by curl fetch) describes SLO's one piece of signature-adjacent hardware in enough operational detail to rule out ASV directly: the Agilis Election Mail Sorting and Processing System (Runbeck) 'quickly sends mail-in ballots past a camera, which scans the ballots for voter signatures, then separates them into groups of 200. If any mail-in ballot envelopes have an abnormality, like a missing signature, that envelope will be separated from the group so poll workers can double-check it. Once those signatures are verified, the ballots are sent through the machine again to be opened.' This is an imaging/sorting machine that FLAGS anomalies for a human poll worker to verify by eye; it does not itself perform algorithmic signature matching (contrast with true ASV products like Parascript, which auto-score the match and only escalate low-confidence cases to a human). No mention of any signature-matching software in county materials, procurement records, or news coverage found in this pass. Confidence held at secondary (both citations are, respectively, an official process page that does not explicitly rule out software and a news description of hardware, not a registrar statement saying 'we do not use ASV'), but this is a firmer, more specific pair of primary-adjacent citations than the previous pass's generic 'absence of evidence' finding (which cited only the vote-by-mail overview page, a page that does not in fact discuss the verification process at all upon re-check).

No in-vote-center mail-ballot signature-verify-and-scan ('Sign, Scan & Go', AB 626) capability. SLO is NOT a Voter's Choice Act county and runs traditional precinct polling places, not vote centers (KSBY e-pollbook coverage repeatedly references voters' 'assigned polling location'). Returned/dropped vote-by-mail ballots are transported to the central Elections Office for scanning, signature verification, and tabulation -- there is no on-site verify-and-scan-at-the-vote-center workflow. AB 626 Sign-Scan-Go is associated with Placer County, not SLO. Finding by absence of evidence in a non-VCA, central-processing county.

San Luis Obispo has NOT adopted the Voter's Choice Act all-mail/vote-center model. SLO does not appear on the CA Secretary of State 'VCA Participating Counties' list, and the county continues to operate traditional precinct-based polling places (where the new 2026 e-pollbooks are being deployed), with vote-by-mail processed centrally at the Elections Office. SLO has been eligible to adopt VCA since Jan 1, 2018 but has not done so.

metrics.denominator: ballots counted to date (CalVoter basis: ballots reported as counted one week after Election Day / ballots counted to date)
metrics.note: chart-read. CalVoter 'Publicly Reported California Ballot Counting Progress on Nov 15, 2022' chart (https://calvoter.org/sites/default/files/2022_ca_county_ballot_counting_speeds.png), % of Nov 8, 2022 ballots reported as counted one week after Election Day; data as of 10:10 AM PT Nov 16, 2022. San Luis Obispo = 59.2%; statewide reference 77.7%.

metrics.denominator: ballots counted to date (CalVoter basis: ballots reported as counted one week after Election Day / ballots counted to date)
metrics.note: chart-read. CalVoter 'Publicly Reported California Ballot Counting Progress on Nov 12, 2024' chart (https://calvoter.org/sites/default/files/2024_ca_county_ballot_counting_speeds.png), % of Nov 5, 2024 ballots reported as counted one week after Election Day; data as of 10:06 PM PT Nov 12, 2024. San Luis Obispo = 64.2%; statewide reference 86.1%.

metrics.denominator: ballots counted to date (CalVoter basis: ballots reported as counted one week after Election Day / ballots counted to date)
metrics.note: Latest odd-year. chart-read. CalVoter 'Publicly Reported California Ballot Counting Progress on Nov 11, 2025' chart (https://calvoter.org/sites/default/files/2025_ca_county_ballot_counting_speeds.png), % of Nov. 4, 2025 ballots counted one week after Election Day. San Luis Obispo = 73.9%; statewide reference 93.9%.

UPDATED (2026-07-09, Task 12): now sourced from data/research/election-night/san-luis-obispo-ca.json (the county-en dataset), computed there per the researching-election-night-share/RUNBOOK methodology, not a different metric. LAST election-night report ('FINAL UNOFFICIAL ELECTION NIGHT RESULTS', generated 11/6/2024 12:10:19 PM PST, 158/158 precincts, Voters Cast 82,548) divided by the certified final (CA SoS SoV, 153,432) = 53.80%.

UPDATED (2026-07-09, Task 12): now sourced from data/research/election-night/san-luis-obispo-ca.json (the county-en dataset). LAST election-night report ('FINAL UNOFFICIAL ELECTION NIGHT RESULTS', generated 11/9/2022 12:28:45 AM PST, 133/133 precincts, Voters Cast 58,096) divided by the certified final (CA SoS SoV, 121,156) = 47.95%.
SLO does not publish per-day/per-release canvass totals in a readily machine-readable form; the day on which >=90% of the certified final was counted cannot be determined.

notes: San Luis Obispo County, CA. TECH: e-pollbooks are NEWLY adopted for the June 2, 2026 Statewide Direct Primary -- the county's first election with electronic pollbooks (it had been a paper-roster holdout); adopted_year=2026 (matches the worklist hint 'e-pollbooks 2026'). E-pollbook VENDOR not yet publicly disclosed; CA SoS only certifies KNOWiNK PollPad and Tenex Precinct Central, so SLO's is presumably one of those (unconfirmed). SLO is NOT a Voter's Choice Act county -> vote-center = not-adopted (primary, via SoS VCA participating-counties list + continued precinct polling). No automated signature verification (ASV not-adopted, secondary; firmed up 2026-07-09 via the county's own EC-3019 signature-cure-letters page plus a detailed KSBY description of the Agilis mail-sorting/imaging machine, which flags anomalies for POLL WORKERS to verify by eye rather than auto-matching) and no in-vote-center Sign-Scan-Go (not-adopted; non-VCA county, central VBM processing). Voting/tabulation system per CA SoS 'Voting Technologies in Use by County' (as of Oct 10, 2025): Dominion ICC 5.10A central tabulation (Central Tabulation Only -- no precinct scanners), Dominion ICX 5.10A ballot-marking devices, Dominion MBP 5.19 mobile ballot printing, and Dominion ImageCast Remote 5.10A RAVBM (recorded as context, not one of the four tracked types). METRICS: oneweek_pct from the canonical CalVoter 'Ballot Processing' per-county charts, chart-read for 2022 (59.2%), 2024 (64.2%) and the latest odd-year 2025 (73.9%) -- all flagged 'chart-read', denominator is CalVoter's ballots-counted-to-date basis; should be re-read precisely in the verification pass. SLO consistently counts slower than the statewide average. electionnight_pct now populated for 2022 (47.95%) and 2024 (53.80%) directly from the county-en dataset (data/research/election-night/san-luis-obispo-ca.json), which Task 12 built with a complete, all-primary, all-CONFIRMED six-election series (2012-2024, 2020 excluded) sourced entirely from SLO's own registrar-posted PDF report series, each one self-titled 'FINAL UNOFFICIAL ELECTION NIGHT RESULTS/SUMMARY' and bracket-proofed against a later canvass report. days_to_90 left null (no per-day canvass totals located). SLO is CONTROL through Nov 2024 (zero tech adopted in that window: no e-pollbook until June 2026, no VCA/vote-center ever, no ASV ever) -- exactly SF-shaped for this dataset's purposes, and used as a second control jurisdiction (packages/data/county_night.json control=true) alongside SF. Verifier: confirm the e-pollbook vendor once 2026 procurement/EAP docs or a post-June-2026 SoS voting-tech list are available.
